message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "single rune yields 33 G (25 base + 2.5 first rounded up + 5 fee = 32.5 -> 33)"
Current error: expected 33, received 5
Passing tests so far: 5

The item type is "rune". Components have base premium 25 G. So 25 + 2.5 = 27.5, then +5 fee = 32.5, Math.ceil → 33.

Minimal implementation: add rune=25 to BASE_PREMIUMS (or treat unknown types as components with 25 base — but spec says unknown types should error later, so just add "rune"=25 explicitly for now).

Run `pnpm test`. Working dir: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text:
All 6 tests pass (5 previous + the new rune test).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added `rune: 25` entry to `BASE_PREMIUMS` in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.ts`
- Result: All tests now pass (6 passing, 30 todo)
- Approach: Minimal addition of a single map entry. The existing `itemPremium` logic already handles the base + 10% first-insurance surcharge + ceiling + processing fee correctly: 25 + 2.5 = 27.5, ceil → 28, + 5 fee = 33. No code logic changes needed.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
